content:
- User asked to run `.ralph/task_switch.sh` to update current tasks.
- Direct execution failed due to missing executable bit; reran with `/bin/bash ./.ralph/task_switch.sh` successfully.
- Important follow-up: after task_switch completed, `.ralph/current_task.txt` was immediately present again and still pointed at deleted bug `.ralph/tasks/bugs/ha-action-deduping-suppresses-retry.md`.
- That means task-switch alone did not clear the stale current-task pointer durably; likely some chooser/external loop repopulated it from stale state. If continuing, inspect whatever is writing `.ralph/current_task.txt` and refresh it from the actual current task inventory.

- Read the saved Choose-Task progress log and the active/completed Ralph task summaries.
- Applied blocker resolution against .ralph/current_tasks_done.md. The only active failing bug is blocked by the remove-backup-feature tasks, so bug-first did not select it yet.
- Chose .ralph/tasks/story-remove-backup-feature/02-task-remove-runtime-restore-bootstrap-and-archive-helper-wiring.md as the next task because it is unblocked, high priority, and its task definition explicitly marks it as the top-priority blocker within the backup-removal story. Wrote that path to .ralph/current_task.txt.
- If resuming later, re-check whether any remove-backup-feature task changed pass state before re-running selection.

- Removed the leading line-1 `---` delimiter from every task markdown file under `.ralph/tasks`.
- Verified the edit was scoped correctly: no `.ralph/tasks/*.md` file still starts with `---`, while interior `---` separators were left untouched.
- Git diff shows 108 task files changed with exactly one deletion each; no tests were needed for this content-only cleanup.
